mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page (`1:118` -> `11:622`), which names button variants with `Style=Primary` and `Type=Contained`. Use as the default mapping for the most prominent action in a section, form, or dialog.
ambiguityRules: If the design reads as text-only emphasis rather than filled emphasis, map to `appearance=text` or `appearance=link` instead.
fallbackRules: If no explicit color role is shown, prefer `variant=primary`.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page, which names the second emphasis tier as `Style=Secondary`. Use when the design shows a branded secondary action rather than the primary blue action.
Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page, which uses `Type=Text` for low-emphasis button styling.

Grounded in the Figma `Button/Link` page, which separates true link patterns from button variants. Use when the design intention is link-like action styling rather than filled or outlined button chrome.
Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page, which models the low-emphasis dark-surface-neutral variant as `Style=Neutral`.
Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page, which models white-on-dark button treatments as `Style=White`.
Grounded in the Figma `Buttons/Main` page, which uses the boolean property `Icon Button=True` for icon-only button variants.
ambiguityRules: Use only when the Figma variant explicitly indicates icon-only treatment.
If the icon appears alongside text, keep `iconButton=false` and use regular left or right icon placement.
fallbackRules: Pair icon-only buttons with tooltip or accessible labeling when the action meaning is not obvious.

mappings.notes: Preferred mapping for compact status, count, or semantic label treatment.
ambiguityRules: If the chip appears dismissible or selection-oriented rather than purely informative, consider `PChip` instead.

mappings.notes: Preferred single-line form-field mapping when the design includes label, helper text, or validation messaging.
ambiguityRules: If the field is clearly a search surface, prefer `PSearchBar`.
If the field is multi-line, prefer `PTextArea`.

mappings.notes: Use built-in `label`, `helperText`, and `errorText` props before composing a separate field wrapper.
Use for notes, descriptions, comments, and other multi-line authored text.
Grounded in the Figma icon and component system, which includes a dedicated `Search` icon and search-field usage separate from generic input entry.
Preferred mapping for labeled form-field selection with single or multi-select behavior.
ambiguityRules: If the design looks like a compact trigger-driven filter menu rather than a form field, prefer `PSelectMenu`.
If the design includes search, checkbox items, `Select All`, or Clear/Apply actions inside an overlay, do not map it to `PSelect`; prefer `PSelectMenu`.

mappings.notes: Preferred mapping for toolbar or filter-trigger overlays with single or multi-selection. This includes Figma nodes that may still be named generically as `Menu` when the behavior is selection-oriented rather than action-oriented.
ambiguityRules: If the node lives on the shared Figma `Menu` page, choose `PSelectMenu` when the content shows search, checkbox rows, `Select All`, secondary text for options, or footer actions like Clear and Apply.
Do not collapse these patterns into `PMenu` just because the surrounding Figma page or frame uses the word `Menu`.

mappings.notes: Use for action lists and flexible dropdown content, especially when the content is not a form field.
ambiguityRules: If the design is primarily about choosing one or more options rather than triggering actions, prefer `PSelectMenu` even if the Figma node is named `Menu`.
Reserve `PMenu` for generic actions, nested action menus, or arbitrary dropdown content without select-menu affordances.

mappings.notes: Preferred mapping for single boolean or tri-state selection controls.
Use when the design shows several related checkbox options under one label or validation message.
Use for short explanatory content only.
Use when the overlay content is richer than tooltip copy and may include sections or actions.
Use for single-person identity markers with image, initials, or status badge support.
Use when several identities need to be shown compactly with overflow handling.
Preferred mapping for full-page Partner layouts with header, side panels, and grid-aligned body content.
ambiguityRules: If the design is only a local section or card layout, do not force `PLayout`.

mappings.notes: Use for semantic tabular data with optional empty, caption, footer, and sorting states.
Use `PTypography` when the Figma layer clearly reflects Partner typography tokens rather than a full interactive component.
ambiguityRules: If the text is helper or validation copy, prefer `variant=helperText`.
If the label belongs to a `PTextInput` or `PTextArea`, use the built-in label prop instead of composing a separate visible label unless the layout demands it.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Typography` page (`10:8314`), which confirms the design-system style names and their direct correspondence to the Partner typography variants.
Grounded in the Figma `Logo` page (`127:21`), which models logos as variants over `Logo`, `Favicon`, and `Brands`. Use `PLogo` rather than hard-coding image assets.
ambiguityRules: Prefer the favicon treatment only when the Figma variant explicitly sets `Favicon=True`.
Map the design-system logo name to the nearest approved `logoName` in the codebase rather than inventing a new asset key.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Icon` page (`1:120` -> `9:5113`), which treats icons as fixed-size assets used inside larger components rather than standalone interactive elements.
fallbackRules: If a specific named Partner icon is not available in the codebase, allow `PIcon` to fall back to the Carbon set rather than creating a custom SVG immediately.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Colors` page (`1:117` -> `7:1191`), which confirms that color roles are token-driven. Agents should prefer token-backed component variants and CSS variables instead of hard-coded values.
ambiguityRules: Treat these as styling-token references, not direct component mappings.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Shadow Effects` page (`1:157` -> `13:1001`), which maps `shadow-sm` to tooltips, `shadow-md` to cards and dropdowns, and `shadow-lg` to large modals and side panels. Prefer component defaults that already align before adding manual shadow overrides.
ambiguityRules: Treat shadows as token guidance, not as a reason to create a custom component variant.

mappings.notes: Grounded in the Figma `Radius` page (`12:861` -> `12:869`), which confirms `radius-sm` for buttons, inputs, and tooltips, `radius-md` for cards and small popups, `radius-lg` for large containers, and `radius-full` for chips and avatars.
ambiguityRules: Treat radius tokens as implementation constraints for existing components rather than direct mappings by themselves.
